title: cinnamomum quadrangulum
intro: Cinnamomum quadrangulum, also known as the East Indian cinnamon or Ceylon cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka. It is a member of the Cinnamomum genus and is characterized by its aromatic bark, which is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The bark of the Cinnamomum quadrangulum plant has been found to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Cinnamomum quadrangulum plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and powdered extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
